questions.question: PPO's key innovation is:
options: Faster computation
A clipped surrogate objective that limits how much the policy can change per update
questions.explanation: Clipping ratio to [1-eps, 1+eps] prevents catastrophically large policy updates.

questions.question: PPO is widely used because:
options: It is the most accurate
It is stable, relatively simple to implement, and works well across diverse tasks
questions.explanation: Used in: ChatGPT/InstructGPT RLHF, OpenAI Five (Dota 2), robotic manipulation.

questions.question: The clipping ratio in PPO clips:
options: Rewards
The probability ratio pi_new/pi_old -- the ratio of new to old action probabilities
questions.explanation: If ratio > 1+eps or < 1-eps, the objective is clipped to prevent large policy shifts.

questions.question: GAE (Generalised Advantage Estimation) in PPO computes:
options: Q-values
A low-variance, low-bias advantage estimate using a weighted combination of TD errors
questions.explanation: GAE(lambda) trades off bias vs variance. lambda=0.95-0.99 works well in practice.

questions.question: PPO uses multiple epochs over the same data to:
options: Reduce memory
Improve sample efficiency -- extract maximum learning from each batch of experience
questions.explanation: Typically 3-4 epochs per rollout batch. More epochs risk policy drift.

questions.question: The value function in PPO is:
options: Not needed
Trained simultaneously with the policy -- used for advantage estimation and as a baseline
questions.explanation: Shared architecture with actor reduces computation. critic_loss = MSE(V(s), returns).

questions.question: PPO vs TRPO (Trust Region Policy Optimisation):
options: Identical
PPO approximates TRPO's constraint with a simpler clipped objective -- easier to implement
questions.explanation: TRPO uses second-order optimisation (expensive). PPO achieves similar results with first-order methods.

questions.question: Reward normalisation in PPO:
options: Reduces reward size only
Normalises reward statistics (running mean/std) to stabilise training across different environments
questions.explanation: Raw rewards vary wildly across environments. Normalisation makes PPO hyperparameters transferable.

questions.question: stable-baselines3 PPO is best used for:
options: Only games
Getting strong baselines quickly on new RL problems -- well-tuned, tested implementation
questions.explanation: from stable_baselines3 import PPO; model = PPO('MlpPolicy', env); model.learn(1M) works immediately.

questions.question: RLHF (Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback) uses PPO to:
options: Train language models from scratch
Fine-tune LLMs to be more helpful and harmless by optimising a reward model trained on human preferences
questions.explanation: ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini all use RLHF/PPO or similar techniques for alignment.
